          /\ timer' <= 2
          /\ state' = state
        \/ /\ state = "Yellow"
          /\ timer' = 0
          /\ state' = "Red"

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, timer>>

prompt:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

The dining philosophers problem with N philosophers and N forks. Each philosopher picks up both adjacent forks to eat, then puts them down.

Hints: Track fork ownership and philosopher states: thinking, hungry, eating.
chosen:
---- MODULE DiningPhilosophers ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N

VARIABLES forks, states

(* Forks: a sequence of length N where forks[i] = j means fork i is held by philosopher j, or forks[i] = -1 if free *)
(* States: a sequence of length N where states[i] = "thinking", "hungry", or "eating" *)

(* Helper functions *)
Left(i) == (i - 1 + N) % N
Right(i) == (i + 1) % N

(* Type invariant *)
TypeOK == 
    /\ forks \in [0..N-1 -> 0..N-1 \cup {-1}]
    /\ states \in [0..N-1 -> {"thinking", "hungry", "eating"}]
    /\ \A i \in 0..N-1: forks[i] = -1 \/ forks[i] \in 0..N-1
    /\ \A i \in 0..N-1: states[i] \in {"thinking", "hungry", "eating"}

(* Initial state: all forks free, all philosophers thinking *)
Init == 
    /\ forks = [i \in 0..N-1 |-> -1]
    /\ states = [i \in 0..N-1 |-> "thinking"]

(* Action: philosopher i picks up left fork if free *)
PickUpLeft(i) == 
    /\ forks[i] = -1
    /\ forks[Left(i)] = -1
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![i] = i, ![Left(i)] = i]
    /\ UNCHANGED states

(* Action: philosopher i picks up right fork if free *)
PickUpRight(i) == 
    /\ forks[i] = -1
    /\ forks[Right(i)] = -1
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![i] = i, ![Right(i)] = i]
    /\ UNCHANGED states

(* Action: philosopher i starts eating if both forks are held by them *)
StartEating(i) == 
    /\ forks[i] = i
    /\ forks[Left(i)] = i
    /\ forks[Right(i)] = i
    /\ states' = [states EXCEPT ![i] = "eating"]
    /\ UNCHANGED forks

(* Action: philosopher i puts down left fork *)
PutDownLeft(i) == 
    /\ forks[i] = i
    /\ forks[Left(i)] = i
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![i] = -1, ![Left(i)] = -1]
    /\ UNCHANGED states

(* Action: philosopher i puts down right fork *)
PutDownRight(i) == 
    /\ forks[i] = i
    /\ forks[Right(i)] = i
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![i] = -1, ![Right(i)] = -1]
    /\ UNCHANGED states

(* Action: philosopher i becomes hungry *)
BecomeHungry(i) == 
    /\ states[i] = "thinking"
    /\ states' = [states EXCEPT ![i] = "hungry"]
    /\ UNCHANGED forks

(* Action: philosopher i stops eating *)
StopEating(i) == 
    /\ states[i] = "eating"
    /\ states' = [states EXCEPT ![i] = "thinking"]
    /\ UNCHANGED forks

(* Next-state relation *)
Next == 
    \E i \in 0..N-1:
        PickUpLeft(i) \/ PickUpRight(i) \/ StartEating(i) \/ PutDownLeft(i) \/ PutDownRight(i) \/ BecomeHungry(i) \/ StopEating(i)

(* Temporal specification *)
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<forks, states>>

====
rejected:
---- MODULE DiningPhilosophers ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N

VARIABLES forks, state

(* State of each philosopher: "thinking", "hungry", or "eating" *)
State == {"thinking", "hungry", "eating"}

(* Initial state: all forks are free (0) and all philosophers are thinking *)
Init == forks = [i \in 1..N |-> 0] /\ state = [i \in 1..N |-> "thinking"]

(* Helper predicate: philosopher i can eat if both adjacent forks are free *)
CanEat(i) == forks[i] = 0 /\ forks[If(i = 1, N, i - 1)] = 0

(* Action: philosopher i becomes hungry *)
Hungry(i) == state[i] = "thinking" /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "hungry"] /\ forks' = forks

(* Action: philosopher i starts eating, picking up both forks *)
Eat(i) == state[i] = "hungry" /\ CanEat(i) /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "eating"] /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![i] = i, ![If(i = 1, N, i - 1)] = i]

(* Action: philosopher i finishes eating, putting down both forks *)
Finish(i) == state[i] = "eating" /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "thinking"] /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![i] = 0, ![If(i = 1, N, i - 1)] = 0]

(* Next-state relation: any philosopher can become hungry, start eating, or finish eating *)
Next == \E i \in 1..N : (Hungry(i) \/ Eat(i) \/ Finish(i))

(* Invariant: no two adjacent philosophers are eating simultaneously *)
NoAdjEating == \A i \in 1..N : state[i] # "eating" \/ state[If(i = 1, N, i - 1)] # "eating"

(* Type invariant: forks is a function from 1..N to 0..N, state is a function from 1..N to State *)
TypeOK == forks \in [1..N -> 0..N] /\ state \in [1..N -> State]

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<forks, state>>

====

prompt:

A simple buffer with one slot. A producer writes to it, a consumer reads from it. The buffer is either empty or full.
chosen:
---- MODULE SingleBuffer ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES buffer, full, empty

Init == 
    /\ buffer = <<>>
    /\ full = FALSE
    /\ empty = TRUE

Next == 
    \/ /\ full = FALSE
       /\ empty = TRUE
       /\ buffer' = Append(buffer, 1)
       /\ full' = TRUE
       /\ empty' = FALSE
    \/ /\ full = TRUE
       /\ empty = FALSE
       /\ buffer' = <<>>
       /\ full' = FALSE
       /\ empty' = TRUE

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<buffer, full, empty>>

prompt:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Dekker's mutual exclusion algorithm for 2 processes, the first known correct solution to the problem.

Hints: VARIABLES turn, wants. Both safety and liveness should be modelled.
chosen:
---- MODULE DekkersAlgorithm ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES turn, wants

Init == /\ turn \in {1, 2}
        /\ wants \in {1, 2, 3}
        /\ turn = 1
        /\ wants = 1

Next == 
  /\ turn' \in {1, 2}
  /\ wants' \in {1, 2, 3}
  /\ (turn = 1 /\ wants = 1) => /\ turn' = 2
                                 /\ wants' = 2
  /\ (turn = 1 /\ wants = 2) => /\ turn' = 1
                                 /\ wants' = 3
  /\ (turn = 1 /\ wants = 3) => /\ turn' = 1
                                 /\ wants' = 3
  /\ (turn = 2 /\ wants = 1) => /\ turn' = 2
                                 /\ wants' = 1
  /\ (turn = 2 /\ wants = 2) => /\ turn' = 2
                                 /\ wants' = 3
  /\ (turn = 2 /\ wants = 3) => /\ turn' = 1
                                 /\ wants' = 3

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<turn, wants>>

====
rejected:

EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

Next == /\ turn' \in {0, 1}

        /\ (wants' = wants \/ wants' = <<1, 0>> \/ wants' = <<0, 1>> \/ wants' = <<1, 1>>)

prompt:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A simple state machine for an ATM: Idle, CardInserted, PINEntered, Dispensing, Done.
chosen:
---- MODULE ATM ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES state, cardInserted, pinEntered, dispensing, done

Init == 
  /\ state = "Idle"
  /\ cardInserted = FALSE
  /\ pinEntered = FALSE
  /\ dispensing = FALSE
  /\ done = FALSE

Next == 
  \/ /\ state = "Idle"
     /\ cardInserted' = FALSE
     /\ pinEntered' = FALSE
     /\ dispensing' = FALSE
     /\ done' = FALSE
     /\ state' = "CardInserted"
  \/ /\ state = "CardInserted"
     /\ cardInserted' = TRUE
     /\ pinEntered' = FALSE
     /\ dispensing' = FALSE
     /\ done' = FALSE
     /\ state' = "PINEntered"
  \/ /\ state = "PINEntered"
     /\ cardInserted' = TRUE
     /\ pinEntered' = TRUE
     /\ dispensing' = FALSE
     /\ done' = FALSE
     /\ state' = "Dispensing"
  \/ /\ state = "Dispensing"
     /\ cardInserted' = TRUE
     /\ pinEntered' = TRUE
     /\ dispensing' = TRUE
     /\ done' = FALSE
     /\ state' = "Done"
  \/ /\ state = "Done"
     /\ cardInserted' = FALSE
     /\ pinEntered' = FALSE
     /\ dispensing' = FALSE
     /\ done' = FALSE
     /\ state' = "Idle"

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, cardInserted, pinEntered, dispensing, done>>

====
rejected:
---- MODULE ATM ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets
CONSTANTS CARD, CASH, PIN

VARIABLES state, card, pin, dispensed


(* --state machine
states
  Idle,
  CardInserted,
  PINEntered,
  Dispensing,
  Done
*)
(* --actions
action
  InsertCard(card) == /\ state = Idle
                    /\ card \in CARD
                    /\ state' = CardInserted
                    /\ card' = card
                    /\ pin' = NULL
                    /\ dispensed' = NULL
  EnterPIN(pin) == /\ state = CardInserted
                   /\ pin \in PIN
                   /\ state' = PINEntered
                   /\ card' = card
                   /\ pin' = pin
                   /\ dispensed' = NULL
  DispenseCash(amount) == /\ state = PINEntered
                         /\ amount \in CASH
                         /\ state' = Dispensing
                         /\ card' = card
                         /\ pin' = pin
                         /\ dispensed' = amount
  Finish == /\ state = Dispensing
           /\ state' = Done
           /\ card' = NULL
           /\ pin' = NULL
           /\ dispensed' = NULL
  Reset == /\ state = Done
           /\ state' = Idle
           /\ card' = NULL
           /\ pin' = NULL
           /\ dispensed' = NULL
*)
(* --variables
*)
(* --type definitions
type
  CARD == 1..10
  PIN == 1..10
  CASH == 1..10
*)
(* --init
init
  /\ state = Idle
  /\ card = NULL
  /\ pin = NULL
  /\ dispensed = NULL
*)
(* --next
next
  \/ /\ state = Idle
     /\ card' = NULL
     /\ pin' = NULL
     /\ dispensed' = NULL
     /\ state' = CardInserted
     /\ card' \in CARD
  \/ /\ state = CardInserted
     /\ card' = card
     /\ pin' \in PIN
     /\ dispensed' = NULL
     /\ state' = PINEntered
  \/ /\ state = PINEntered
     /\ card' = card
     /\ pin' = pin
     /\ dispensed' \in CASH
     /\ state' = Dispensing
  \/ /\ state = Dispensing
     /\ card' = card
     /\ pin' = pin
     /\ dispensed' = NULL
     /\ state' = Done
  \/ /\ state = Done
     /\ card' = NULL
     /\ pin' = NULL
     /\ dispensed' = NULL
     /\ state' = Idle
*)
(* --type invariants
type
  TypeOK == /\ state \in {Idle, CardInserted, PINEntered, Dispensing, Done}
           /\ card \in CARD \/ card = NULL
           /\ pin \in PIN \/ pin = NULL
           /\ dispensed \in CASH \/ dispensed = NULL
*)
(* --specification
spec
  Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars
*)
(* --tla configuration
config
  SPECIFICATION Spec
  INVARIANT TypeOK
*)

A readers-writers lock where multiple readers can hold the lock simultaneously but writers are exclusive.
chosen:
---- MODULE ReadersWriters ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

VARIABLES readers, writers, waitingWriters

Init == /\ readers = 0
        /\ writers = 0
        /\ waitingWriters = 0

Next == \/ /\ readers > 0
          /\ readers' = readers - 1
          /\ writers' = writers
          /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters
        \/ /\ readers = 0
          /\ writers = 0
          /\ waitingWriters > 0
          /\ readers' = 0
          /\ writers' = 1
          /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters - 1
        \/ /\ readers = 0
          /\ writers = 0
          /\ waitingWriters = 0
          /\ readers' = readers + 1
          /\ writers' = writers
          /\ waitingWriters' = waitingWriters

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<readers, writers, waitingWriters>>
